# Website Builder — Design Intelligence Skill

## Origin & Attribution

**Source:** [github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill](https://github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill)  
**Cloned to:** `sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/` (March 2026)  
**License:** See `sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/LICENSE`  
**Upstream version:** v2.0 (161 reasoning rules)

This is a searchable design intelligence database packaged as an AI skill. It provides a BM25 + regex reasoning engine that analyses a product description and outputs a complete, industry-specific design system in seconds — covering layout patterns, UI style, colour palette, typography, key CSS effects, and explicit anti-patterns to avoid.

---

## What It Contains

| Dataset | Count | Description |
|---------|-------|-------------|
| UI Styles | 67 | Full CSS keywords, AI prompts, implementation checklists, Tailwind ratings, design tokens |
| Industry reasoning rules | 161 | Maps product type → recommended patterns, style, colours, fonts, anti-patterns |
| Colour palettes | 161 | 1:1 mapped to the 161 product types |
| Font pairings | 57 | With Google Fonts import strings and CSS `@import` |
| Chart types | 25 | With library recommendations (Recharts, D3, Chart.js) |
| Tech stack guides | 13 | React, Next.js, Astro, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, SwiftUI, Flutter, shadcn, Jetpack Compose, React Native, HTML+Tailwind |
| UX guidelines | 99 | Best practices + accessibility rules + anti-patterns |

**Script location:** `sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py`  
**Dependency:** `rank-bm25` (already in `requirements.txt`)

---

## How It Fits Into the Agentop Webgen Pipeline

The standard pipeline is:

```
ClientBrief → SitePlannerAgent → PageGeneratorAgent → QA/SEO/AEO
```

This skill adds a **Design System step** before planning, so the LLM receives
industry-grounded decisions rather than improvising style:

```
ClientBrief
    │
    ▼
[THIS SKILL] Generate Design System
    │  Outputs: pattern, style, colours, fonts, effects, anti-patterns
    │
    ▼
SitePlannerAgent  ← inject design system as context before planning prompt
    │
    ▼
PageGeneratorAgent  ← pass design tokens + CSS keywords into _generate_global_css()
    │
    ▼
QA / SEO / AEO agents
```

**Concrete integration points:**

1. **Before `SitePlannerAgent.run()`** — call `--design-system` with the `ClientBrief.business_name` and `business_type` as the query. Inject the full output into the planning LLM prompt as a `<design_system>` block.

2. **Inside `PageGeneratorAgent._generate_global_css()`** — pass the style's `CSS/Technical Keywords` and design token variables as additional context. The LLM will produce far more targeted, consistent CSS.

3. **Per-client persistence** — use `--persist` to write `design-system/MASTER.md` inside the client's folder under `clients/`. Each page override goes in `design-system/pages/<slug>.md`.

4. **Anti-pattern enforcement** — the reasoning rules include explicit anti-patterns per industry (e.g., "no AI purple/pink gradients for banking", "no dark mode for wellness spas"). Pass these to QA agent as a checklist.

---

## Hard Design Constraints

These rules apply to every site generated by Agentop regardless of industry, client, or style. They are non-negotiable unless the client explicitly overrides a specific rule.

### Colour
- **Maximum 5 colours total**: 1 primary, 2–3 neutrals, 1–2 accents. Never exceed 5 without explicit client permission.
- **No purple/violet as a prominent colour** unless explicitly requested. It reads as generic AI output.
- **No gradients by default.** Solid colours only. If a gradient is required: max 3 stops, analogous colours only (e.g., blue→teal), never complementary (e.g., pink→green).

### Typography
- **Maximum 2 font families.** One heading family (multiple weights are fine), one body family. Never mix more.
- Use Google Fonts only for families confirmed to load in the target font pairing output.

### Images & Icons
- **No placeholder images.** Either generate real images or source from a royalty-free library. A grey box saying "image here" is never acceptable in a deliverable.
- **No emojis as icons or decorative elements.** Use an icon library (Lucide, Heroicons, Phosphor) or SVGs.

### Tailwind
- **No arbitrary values** (`p-[16px]`, `w-[420px]`). Use only the Tailwind spacing/sizing scale (`p-4`, `w-96`).
- **Never use `space-x-*` or `space-y-*`** for component spacing — use `gap-*` inside a flex/grid container instead.

### Overall Aesthetic
- Ship something **interesting rather than boring, but never ugly**. A visually bland page that technically works is a failure. An ambitious page that looks bad is a failure. The target is in that quadrant together: distinctive + polished.

---

## Pre-Build Design Brief (Mandatory)

Before writing any HTML, CSS, or component code, `SitePlannerAgent` must emit a structured design brief. This brief gates `PageGeneratorAgent` — code generation does not begin until the brief is confirmed.

**Required brief fields:**

```
DESIGN BRIEF
============
Client:       <name>
Industry:     <type>

Colour Palette:
  Primary:    #XXXXXX  (<name>)
  Secondary:  #XXXXXX  (<name>)
  Accent:     #XXXXXX  (<name>)
  Background: #XXXXXX  (<name>)
  Text:       #XXXXXX  (<name>)

Typography:
  Heading:    <Family>, weights: <400,700>
  Body:       <Family>, weights: <400,500>
  Google Fonts import: <full URL>

Page Structure:
  1. <Section name> — <purpose>
  2. <Section name> — <purpose>
  ...

Mood / Visual Direction:
  <2-3 sentence description of the aesthetic direction>

Anti-Patterns to Avoid:
  - <industry-specific anti-pattern 1>
  - <industry-specific anti-pattern 2>
```

Run `--design-system` (Step 1 below) to populate this brief from the BM25 engine before filling it in.

---

## Asset Generation Order

Generate ALL image/media assets **only after all code files are created**, in a single batch at the end of the pipeline — not inline while writing components. This prevents blocking code generation on slow image jobs.

When re-building or updating an existing site, **reuse existing assets whenever possible** — check `clients/<name>/assets/` before triggering new generation.

---

## Navigation Integration Mandate

When a new page or route is added to any site, **always update the navigation structure** in the same task — navbar links, sidebar items, footer links, and sitemap. A page that exists but is unreachable from navigation is a broken deliverable.

---

## Prerequisites

```bash
# Python must be available (it is — we use it everywhere in the backend)
python3 --version

# rank-bm25 must be installed (already added to requirements.txt)
pip install rank-bm25
```

---

## Core Usage

### Step 1 — Generate a Full Design System (always start here)

```bash
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "<business_type> <industry> <keywords>" \
  --design-system \
  -p "Client / Project Name"
```

**Examples:**

```bash
# Restaurant / food service
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "restaurant food delivery local service" --design-system -p "Bella Italia"

# SaaS landing page
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "SaaS B2B project management tool modern" --design-system -p "TaskFlow"

# Beauty / spa
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "beauty spa wellness premium service" --design-system -p "Luxe Spa"

# E-commerce
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "ecommerce fashion luxury boutique" --design-system -p "StyleHouse"
```

The output is a formatted block containing:
- **PATTERN** — recommended landing page structure with section order
- **STYLE** — UI style name + keywords + CSS implementation notes
- **COLORS** — primary, secondary, CTA, background, text with hex values
- **TYPOGRAPHY** — font pair + Google Fonts import URL
- **KEY EFFECTS** — CSS/animation guidance
- **AVOID** — industry-specific anti-patterns
- **PRE-DELIVERY CHECKLIST** — accessibility + UX verification items

### Step 2 (optional) — Persist the Design System

```bash
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "<query>" --design-system --persist -p "Project Name"
```

Creates:
- `design-system/MASTER.md` — global source of truth for this project
- `design-system/pages/<pagename>.md` — page-specific overrides (add `--page "about"`)

### Step 3 (optional) — Targeted Domain Searches

After getting the design system, use these for additional detail:

```bash
# More style options
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "glassmorphism dark" --domain style

# Colour palette only
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "healthcare calm trust" --domain color

# Font pairings
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "elegant luxury serif" --domain typography

# Landing page patterns
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "hero social-proof conversion" --domain landing

# UX best practices / anti-patterns
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "animation accessibility loading" --domain ux

# Chart type recommendations
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "real-time monitoring dashboard" --domain chart
```

### Step 4 (optional) — Stack-Specific Guidelines

```bash
# Next.js (our primary frontend stack)
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "performance bundle lazy loading" --stack nextjs

# HTML + Tailwind (webgen output)
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "responsive grid component" --stack html-tailwind
```

---

## Domain Reference

| Domain flag | Use for | Example query |
|-------------|---------|---------------|
| `product` | Product type recommendations | `"SaaS B2B dashboard"` |
| `style` | UI style selection | `"minimalism modern clean"` |
| `color` | Colour palettes | `"fintech trust professional"` |
| `typography` | Font pairings | `"playful modern rounded"` |
| `landing` | Page structure / CTA strategies | `"hero testimonials pricing"` |
| `chart` | Chart type + library | `"trend over time comparison"` |
| `ux` | Best practices + anti-patterns | `"hover animation scroll"` |

---

## Trigger Scenarios

Invoke this skill when the user asks for any of the following:

| User request | Action |
|---|---|
| "Build a landing page for X" | Run `--design-system` with business description |
| "Generate a website for a [industry] business" | Run `--design-system` with industry keywords |
| "What style / colours should I use for X?" | Run `--design-system` or domain-specific search |
| "Create a pricing page / hero section" | Run `--domain style` + `--domain landing` |
| "Review this page for UX issues" | Run `--domain ux` with relevant keywords |
| "What fonts work for a luxury brand?" | Run `--domain typography "luxury elegant"` |
| "Add charts to a dashboard" | Run `--domain chart` with data type |

---

## Example End-to-End Workflow

**Task:** Generate a website for a local solicitors firm.

```bash
# 1. Generate the design system
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "legal solicitors law firm professional trust" --design-system -p "Harrison & Co Solicitors"
```

**Output will specify** (actual results vary by search):
- Pattern: Trust & Authority (certifications, case studies)
- Style: Swiss Modernism 2.0 or Minimalism (professional, grid-based)
- Colors: Navy + white + gold accent (trust palette)
- Typography: Cormorant Garamond + Inter (authoritative + readable)
- Anti-patterns: No glassmorphism, no vibrant neons, no playful shapes

```bash
# 2. Persist for the project
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "legal solicitors law firm professional trust" \
  --design-system --persist \
  -p "Harrison & Co Solicitors"

# 3. Get additional UX rules for professional services
python3 sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py \
  "accessibility contrast professional forms" --domain ux
```

Then pass the full design system output to `SitePlannerAgent` and `PageGeneratorAgent` as context.

---

## Updating the Skill

The cloned repo in `sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/` is the live copy. To update:

```bash
cd sandbox/ui-ux-pro-max-skill && git pull
```

The CSV data files in `src/ui-ux-pro-max/data/` are the source of truth and are updated by the upstream maintainer as new styles and industry categories are added.
